what_input_feature: The same GPOS-attachment font; the winning edge is that the call-context-indexed coverage map treats reaching the GPOS/shaping functions via new call paths as new coverage, rewarding inputs that drive shaping deeper.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 and hits the branch 166 times; its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so each (call-context, edge) pair behind the deep shaping call chain (hops 2-8 in/around position_finish_offsets) counts as new coverage and keeps inputs that progress through it.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (15 hits) because its bare edge-bucket signal saturates on the shared shaping edges and gives no extra reward for reaching them through new call contexts, so it stalls before the attachment flag is set.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) buckets via MaxMapFeedback) gives finer reward along the deep GPOS call chain, an advantage naive's context-free edge map lacks.

function: hb-buffer-verify.cc:buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break(hb_buffer_t*, hb_buffer_t*, hb_font_t*, hb_feature_t const*, unsigned int, char const* const*)
what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a valid font shaping a multi-glyph buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph that makes line 124's mask test true.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 2615; its CtxHook folds the call-context into the edge-map index so reaching the line-124/shaping code via the recursive hb_shape_full -> verify -> buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break path counts as new (call-context, edge) coverage, retaining intermediate inputs that drive deeper into the verify machinery.
why_loser_doesnt: plain naive collapses these distinct call paths onto the same bare edges, blocks 8/10, and never sets the mask true (line 124 T=0 F=180); its loser seeds are short and never re-enter the shaping path with the context diversity that would expose the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph state.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) pairs) preserves inputs that reach the deep recursive shaping/verify path, giving extra corpus retention that naive's bare-edge feedback lacks, so the winner reaches the line-124 true side.

what_input_feature: Same structural requirement (a table whose parse drives init with table_index==1), but the winner benefits because reaching init() through distinct call contexts (iter_input vs iter_context, CFF dict paths) counts as new coverage.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 with avg hitcount 30402 vs loser 2810; init() is invoked from multiple callers (iter_input.init(this,false) and iter_context.init(this,true) at lines 735-736, plus CFF cff2 dict paths), so the context-indexed map rewards each (call-context, edge) pair and retains the seeds that drive deeper into layout matching.
why_loser_doesnt: naive collapses all calls to init() into one bare edge bucket, so once that edge is hit it earns no further corpus credit for reaching it via the GPOS-resolving context, and stays blocked 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same init() edge reached through the GPOS-table call path is a NEW (call-context, edge) bucket; MaxMapFeedback preserves those inputs, building a corpus that explores the table-driven contexts naive's bare-edge feedback ignores.

what_input_feature: An input that drives the layout machinery deep enough (matcher/skipping-iterator/ChainContext dispatch) that the constructor at table_index==1 with num_coords is reached on a call path distinct from shallow parsing.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx records (call-context, edge) pairs as new coverage; the same downstream functions (matcher_t setters, skipping_iterator_t, ChainContext::dispatch) reached via the GPOS-apply call path register as fresh coverage, rewarding the deeper exploration the blocker needs.
why_loser_doesnt: naive sees only bare edges, so revisiting matcher/iterator code via a deeper call path yields no new coverage and the seeds that would reach the table_index==1 constructor are not retained.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the layout-apply functions through a new call context counts as new coverage and steers corpus toward the deeper GPOS-apply path.

what_input_feature: A font that reaches the kern-table path through the same edges naive hits but under a call context that registers as new coverage, sustaining corpus entries that drive deeper into hb_ot_shape_planner_t::compile toward the blocker.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain (compile <- init0 <- create2 ...) into the edge-map index, so edges in the kern-decision block at L168-L187 are recorded as new (call-context, edge) pairs, keeping more intermediate font states alive and exploring the false side.
why_loser_doesnt: naive uses bare edge buckets, so once the L185-L187 edges are hit once under any context they no longer yield new coverage; the loser's 0x20-padded inputs never accrue the structural variety needed and it remains blocked (resolved 2/10).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes edges by call context, turning the same kern-decision edge into multiple distinct coverage slots and thereby preserving inputs that incrementally build the valid kern table that flips the branch false.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_consonant_syllable(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: A glyph buffer that, after Indic categorization, produces a syllable where the backward base-consonant scan hits a non-consonant glyph that is a ZWJ (info[i].indic_category()==ZWJ) with start<i AND the glyph one position earlier is a Halant (info[i-1].indic_category()==I_Cat(H)). This requires a specific multi-glyph ordering (..., H, ZWJ, ...) inside one syllable reached via the consonant-syllable / standalone-cluster reordering path.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the true side in 10/10 trials with avg 18 hits on the branch; the CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index, so the line-584 edge reached via initial_reordering_consonant_syllable's two callers (standalone_cluster at line 960, myanmar at line 314) counts as distinct new coverage, keeping the reordering region rewarding and letting havoc keep building the H,ZWJ glyph ordering. The winner seeds carry 'uu-han'/'-han' ASCII runs and dense 0x0d/0x20 glyph-id bytes that map into the joiner/halant categories.
why_loser_doesnt: naive sees the same bare edge regardless of which caller reached it, so once the reordering edges are bucketed it stops getting coverage reward there; it resolves true only 2/10 (blocked 8/10, avg 1 hit, avg 16.60h blocked) and never assembles the H-immediately-before-ZWJ ordering, taking the false side 12 times at L 584 (T=0 F=12).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook (HookableInProcessExecutor) makes a (call-context, edge) pair the unit of new coverage instead of a bare edge. The same reordering code reached from consonant_syllable vs standalone_cluster vs myanmar yields fresh map slots, so the fuzzer keeps energy on the syllable-reordering region and havoc eventually lands the H,ZWJ glyph sequence the true branch needs. This is context-sensitive coverage, not any comparison-solving technique.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:final_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: An input decoded into an Indic syllable with at least 2 glyphs where, after base-finding, the base glyph's indic_position() exceeds POS_BASE_C and start < base — i.e. multi-consonant clusters that drive the final_reordering machinery past the simple-base case into the matra/reph reordering paths.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seeds are dense repeated-byte glyph runs (e.g. 22-byte seed of repeated 0x75 'u' plus 0x97 runs) that decode to long uniform syllables, exercising the deep reordering paths; naive_ctx folds the call-context into the edge map, so reaching L1099 via the specific syllable-segmentation/base-finding call path registers as NEW coverage and is retained, repeatedly steering mutation toward the rare base>POS_BASE_C configuration (winner resolved=10/10).
why_loser_doesnt: The loser (naive) sees only the bare edge at L1099, which it already covers on the False side (T=0 F=17 in one variant), so the rare True side earns no novelty credit; its blocking seeds are sparse 0x09/0x0c/0x00 patterns that stay in the simple-base / no-pre-base-matra regime and it blocks 8/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds a running hash of the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same L1099 edge counts as distinct coverage per call context; this context-sensitive coverage rewards seeds that reach the deep reordering path through new call contexts, driving naive_ctx to retain and extend them while plain naive gives the bare edge no novelty.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:final_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Bytes decoding to an Indic syllable where the pre-base matra back-scan (L1152-1154) lands on a halant whose immediately following glyph at new_pos+1 has indic_category ZWJ, so info[new_pos + 1].indic_category() == I_Cat(ZWJ) is true. This requires a halant+ZWJ sequence positioned correctly within a non-Malayalam/Tamil syllable, reached only after the deep reordering prologue (base-finding, matra-reorder guard) succeeds.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in 10/10 trials with avg hitcount 14 on the branch; its per-role coverage drives the true-side block at L1171-1177 ([W] new_pos--; goto search). The CtxHook folds the call context into the edge map, so each foreach_syllable invocation of final_reordering_syllable_indic is recorded as a distinct (call-context, edge) bucket, rewarding inputs that exercise the reordering path under varied syllable contexts and so reaching the ZWJ-after-halant arm.
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 2/10 (blocked 8/10) with avg hitcount 1; at L1170 it records W(T/F) 21/1 vs L(T/F) 0/21 — naive never takes the true side. Naive's bare per-edge MaxMapFeedback treats the blocker edge as already-covered once any syllable reaches L1170 false, so it gets no new-coverage reward for steering toward the rare halant+ZWJ configuration and stalls (avg duration blocked 21.40h vs winner 4.80h).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook context-sensitive coverage: the same L1170 edge is logged at different map slots per call context (per syllable / call path), so distinct syllable arrangements count as new coverage and the corpus accumulates inputs that probe the deep reordering arm until one satisfies the ZWJ category test. Naive lacks this granularity (identical havoc/token mutators, no I2S, no CMP_MAP), so the divergence is attributable to the single technique delta: context-sensitive coverage.

source_line: return (info.use_category() == USE(H) || info.use_category() == USE(HVM) || info.use_category() == USE(IS)) &&
what_input_feature: A USE-shaper syllable whose glyph at a reorder index carries use_category H, HVM, or IS and is not ligated, so the is_halant_use true branch fires; is_halant_use is invoked from reorder_syllable_use at two distinct call sites (lines 401 and 425), so the same blocker edge is reachable through multiple call paths.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the true branch in all 10 trials, hitting it 76 times on average; the per-role coverage overlay shows the loser-blocking seeds reach line 357 with T=33 (true side taken 33 times) while never satisfying it during the loser run, and the CtxHook indexes each (call-context, edge) pair separately so reaching the halant test via a fresh call path registers as new coverage and is retained, driving the corpus toward the H/HVM/IS-category configuration.
why_loser_doesnt: plain naive blocks the true branch in 8 of 10 trials with avg hitcount 0; its bare edge-bucket feedback cannot distinguish reaching is_halant_use via different call sites, so it never builds the corpus pressure to discover an input whose reorder-position glyph category is H/HVM/IS and unligated.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook (HookableInProcessExecutor) folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same is_halant_use edge is recorded at different map slots per caller chain; because is_halant_use is called from reorder_syllable_use at two sites, context-sensitive coverage manufactures extra 'new bucket' signals that retain partial-progress seeds plain naive discards, eventually solving the H/HVM/IS category condition.

what_input_feature: A glyph whose codepoint equals 0x0B05 must reach buffer->cur() inside the HB_SCRIPT_ORIYA arm; bytes 05 0b (little-endian 0x0B05) at the leading codepoint slot, sitting on the same Oriya call-context that the surrounding indic shaper exercises.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 trials. Winning seeds open with `05 0b 00 00` (e.g. seed cdda5341, fuzzer=naive_ctx), placing 0x0B05 at the current glyph. The CtxHook folds the Oriya call-context into the edge-map slot so even a single visit to case 0x0B05u becomes a brand-new (call-context, edge) bucket, rewarding seeds that drive the matched-then-_output_with_dotted_circle path.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10. Its loser seeds carry 0x0B0F (`0f 0b ...`) or noise (`c8 20 0b 0b`, `15 bd cb cb`) at the codepoint slot, landing on the sister case 0x0B0Fu/0x0B13u line 199 (L=2 true) instead of 0x0B05u (L=0 true). Without context-indexing, naive sees the bare ORIYA edges as already-covered and stops paying energy to refine toward 0x0B05.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook makes coverage context-sensitive: the same case-0x0B05u edge counts as new coverage per distinct call context, so the shaper-dispatch path that reaches it is rewarded and retained where plain edge coverage saturates.

what_input_feature: A buffer whose script resolves to HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA and that contains a glyph whose codepoint == 0x0D85 (little-endian bytes 85 0d) at a position where buffer->idx + 1 < count, so the inner per-glyph switch reaches the true side of case 0x0D85u at line 295.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the repeated 4-byte pattern 85 0d 00 00 (codepoint 0x0D85), and the per-role overlay marks line 295 case 0x0D85u as [W] winner-resolving only; the winner takes the true side 15 times (W T=15) at the blocker line.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry sibling Sinhala codepoints (91 0d = 0x0D91, ca 0d) but never 0x0D85, so the inner switch lands on case 0x0D91u (marked [B], both hit) and the blocker case 0x0D85u stays at T=0; the byte diff shows offset 0x0000 W=85 vs L=91/ca.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deeply nested per-glyph switch arm via the Sinhala for-loop registers as fresh (call-context, edge) coverage that bare naive's flat edge map already considers seen; this extra granularity rewards the corpus growth that drives the buffer toward a 0x0D85 glyph, resolving the branch in 10/10 trials vs 1/10 for naive.

what_input_feature: A font/buffer input that selects HB_SCRIPT_MODI and presents a current-glyph codepoint of 0x11600u or 0x11601u (the leading Modi vowel) at the vowel-constraint scan position, so the true side of the case at line 432 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in 8/10 trials and its winner-resolving seeds carry the 16 01 byte run (e.g. seed 655f90fb at offset 0x0a: 6a 01 16 01) that encodes the 0x11600/0x11601 codepoint reaching the MODI case true side, where W shows T=5 on the blocker line.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 8/10 trials; its loser-blocking seeds reach the MODI switch (case 432 is [B]) but only fall through the false side (L shows T=0 F=75 on one blocker row) and never present the 0x11600/0x11601 codepoint at the scan position.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same MODI-loop edge counts as new coverage when reached via a different shaper call path; this finer (call-context, edge) granularity preserves intermediate seeds along the deep preprocess_text_use -> _hb_preprocess_text_vowel_constraints chain that plain naive's bare-edge feedback discards, letting it accumulate the codepoint that takes the true side.

what_input_feature: A glyph buffer whose script is HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI containing an adjacent pair: current codepoint 0x0906 (case at line 74) followed by next-glyph codepoint in {0x093A, 0x0945, 0x0946, 0x0947, 0x0948}, which makes the inner switch at line 75 take the true case-arm at line 77.
why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner-resolving seed carries repeated little-endian 0x0906 / 0x0945 codepoint words near offsets 0x10-0x21 (06 09 00 00 ... 45 09 00 00), supplying both the 0x0906 head glyph and a 0x0945-family follower; on the winner the blocker line 77 records T=1 (true side taken) versus the loser's T=0.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds reach the 0x0906 case (line 74 F=28) and execute the inner switch, but the follower glyph never lands in {0x093A,0x0945,0x0946,0x0947,0x0948}, so line 77 stays at T=0 F=2 and the true branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same vowel-constraint edge reached via the Indic preprocess_text call path becomes a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket; this rewards the scheduler for keeping/mutating Devanagari inputs that drive deeper into the per-script switch, letting it hill-climb to the specific 0x0906+follower pair, whereas plain naive sees only a bare already-covered edge and gets no signal to refine the second codepoint.

what_input_feature: The decoded glyph buffer must (1) select the HB_SCRIPT_ORIYA switch arm and (2) at some loop position have a current codepoint equal to 0x0B0F or 0x0B13, i.e. the input must encode the exact little-endian glyph bytes 0F 0B or 13 0B at a 4-byte glyph slot so buffer->cur().codepoint hits that case label.
why_winner_satisfies: Every naive_ctx winner seed is built from repeated 4-byte glyph records and several carry the literal 0f 0b / 13 0b codepoint pattern: winner seed 89dfff878e1186bc and 61a1738ca6c821b1 both begin '13 0b 00 00', placing 0x0B13 as a current glyph; the per-role overlay shows the winner takes line 199 TRUE 15 times (T=15 F=60) while the loser never does (T=0).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds reach the ORIYA loop but their glyph bytes never present 0x0B0F/0x0B13 as a current codepoint at line 199 (loser T=0 F=150 on the TRUE-deciding case row); their bytes are scattered high/ASCII values (e.g. cb cb cb, 72 61 66 'raf', 6f6f6f) so the codepoint match never fires and the matched flag stays false.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller-chain hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deeply nested per-codepoint case inside the ORIYA loop under this specific call context registers as a NEW (call-context, edge) bucket that bare naive never distinguishes; this finer coverage granularity keeps the seeds that march the buffer index toward the 0x0B0F/0x0B13 codepoint, letting naive_ctx resolve the true side while naive sees no novelty and abandons it.

what_input_feature: An input parsed into an HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA buffer whose current glyph codepoint equals one of 0x0D8B, 0x0D8F, or 0x0D94 (the line-303 switch arm), distinct from the 0x0D91 arm the loser settles into. Byte-level this means the little-endian codepoint bytes 8b/8f/94 followed by 0d appear at the parsed glyph offset.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner naive_ctx seeds carry the exact 8f 0d and 8d 0d little-endian Sinhala codepoint byte-pairs at parsed glyph offsets (e.g. seed 1 '8f 8f 8f 0d', seed 2 '8d 0d ... 8f 0d', seed 4 '8f 0d 00 00 8d 0d'); the per-role overlay marks line 303 [W] (winner-resolving only) and the divergent-branch table shows W taking the true side T=12 while L never does (T=0).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds reach the SINHALA switch but land on the 0x0D91 arm (line 309 marked [L]); e.g. loser seed 1 is the 2-byte '91 0d', i.e. codepoint 0x0D91, which routes to line 309 not line 303. The overlay marks lines 303-307 [W] and lines 309-316 [L], and the divergent-branch table shows L at line 303 with T=0 (true side never taken).
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx differs from naive only by the CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same line-303 edge is a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket when reached via the USE/Indic preprocess_text call path. That extra granularity rewards a seed that drives the Sinhala arm, retaining and mutating it toward the 0x0D8B/0x0D8F/0x0D94 codepoints; plain naive collapses the edge to a single bare-edge bucket already satisfied by the sibling 0x0D91 arm, so it never gets corpus pressure to discover line 303.

what_input_feature: Same Sinhala-script buffer with current glyph U+0D91, but reached and rewarded because the CtxHook indexes the edge by call context, making the (caller-chain, edge) pair fresh coverage that survives into the corpus.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same vowel-constraints edges count as new coverage when reached through different shaper call paths (preprocess_text_use / preprocess_text_indic), giving extra corpus retention that drives it to resolve all 10 trials with 85 avg hits; its resolving seeds (naive_ctx seeds 1,4,5) carry the 91 0d codepoint and contextual byte runs.
why_loser_doesnt: naive collapses every reach of the edge to one map slot regardless of call context, so once the bare edge is seen there is no new-coverage incentive to refine inputs toward the 0x0D91 arm; it blocks for the full 16.00h.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook context-sensitive coverage records (call-context, edge) pairs as new buckets, retaining more intermediate seeds along the shaper call chain and thereby exploring the Sinhala switch arms that bare-edge naive abandons.

what_input_feature: A SQL input building a deeply nested expression tree (recursive SELECT with many nested '(' groups and chained '+' operators) so that heightOfExprList is invoked from many distinct call contexts and its per-element loop continues past the first item, taking the false (loop-continuation/exit) branch at line 100230.
why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner seeds are 297-619 byte inputs all prefixed 'SELECT' with stacked '(' groups and long '+' runs (e.g. 'SELECT+...+PSELE(+++S...((((((SELECT'), which build a tall expression tree; the blocker line records W T=13 F=20 (false side hit 20x) and the function fires heavily (W hits 392 on branch, deep call chain through sqlite3Select/sqlite3PExpr/sqlite3ExprAttachSubtrees).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are tiny / shallow ('vacuum', ';vacuum;', and a flat 'SELECT+SE,++LE...' with no nesting depth) that never build a multi-element expression list deep enough to re-enter the loop, so the blocker line shows L T=16 F=0 — the false branch is never taken and naive resolves 0/10.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same heightOfExprList edge recorded under a deeper/different recursive call path counts as new coverage. This rewards incremental deepening of the expression tree (each new recursion context is a new bucket), guiding the fuzzer to grow nesting until the list loop runs >1 iteration and falls through the false side. Plain naive sees only the bare edge, so once heightOfExprList's first edge is hit it gets no further coverage signal for deeper nesting and stalls on the true side.

what_input_feature: An input that reaches the yy_reduce reducer at 157646 via the deep SELECT-parse call context (sqlite3RunParser -> sqlite3Parser -> yy_reduce), so the edge is newly interesting under context-indexed coverage.
why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winner seeds begin with 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) and drive yy_reduce to hit the blocker true side 11 times with avg hitcount 140 on the branch, far above the loser's 3.
why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds resolve the branch in only 2/10 trials with avg hitcount 3, never sustaining the deep parse call path that would make the reducer edge a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage event.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the running caller-chain hash into the edge-map index, so the yy_reduce reducer edge reached through the SELECT-expression call chain registers as a new (call-context, edge) bucket; this keeps intermediate SELECT-shaped inputs in the corpus and lets naive_ctx incrementally extend the parse where bare-edge naive coverage sees nothing new.

what_input_feature: SQL text that parses to the grammar rule expr ::= LP select RP, i.e. a '(' opening a nested SELECT statement closed by ')' in an expression position, so yy_reduce dispatches the case-209 reduce action at line 157866.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin 'SELECT' and contain a '(' followed by a second nested 'SELE'/'SELECT' token (e.g. '(+++S...((((((SELECT'), giving the parser a parenthesized inner SELECT that reduces via LP select RP; the true side is taken 10 times under W.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short fragments (15-79 bytes) with no parenthesized inner SELECT and never reach yy_reduce case 209 at all (the whole yy_reduce body shows T=0 throughout L), so the true side is taken 0 times.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deep parser reduce path along the renameParseSql/sqlite3RunParser/sqlite3Parser/yy_reduce call chain registers as new (call-context, edge) coverage; this rewards the intermediate parser states that build a nested-SELECT expression, whereas bare-edge naive coverage saturates on the same edges seen via shallower paths and never retains the inputs that drive toward the LP select RP reduction.

what_input_feature: A syntactically rich SQL input (leading SELECT keyword followed by nested parser productions) that drives the Lemon parser through deep yy_reduce reductions, so yy_destructor is invoked from a destructor-bearing grammar symbol and takes the true branch at line 155710.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal bytes 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) and contain repeated '(' and '$' structure that drives many parser reductions; the blocker true side fires (L155710 T=8 in W) and yy_reduce executes 2680 false / hundreds of true reductions only in W, reaching the destructor.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed fragments (15-79 bytes) that do not begin with SELECT and never drive the parser into the deep reduction paths; the blocker true side is never taken (L155710 T=0 in L) and yy_reduce sees only 61 reductions.
mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same yy_destructor edge recorded under a new (call-context, edge) pair counts as new coverage; this rewards inputs that reach yy_destructor via novel parse-reduction call paths, which plain naive collapses into one already-seen edge and so never prioritizes. Both fuzzers share identical havoc/token mutators with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the only enabling difference is coverage granularity.
